name: Progress Comparison Protocol (PR-F2, handover F-006)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-006. Author ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md: the reusable audit-to-audit / baseline-to-baseline progress comparison protocol. Per .agent/feature_list_005.json feature 036 and product_spec_005.md FR-09, a comparison takes two approved baselines or audits (ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, and the audit protocol ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md which feature 035 authors) as inputs and MUST distinguish new / resolved / reopened / stale findings, MUST close a resolved finding only with closure evidence, MUST NOT silently reuse stale evidence as current, and MUST make every score movement traceable to the specific findings and evidence responsible. Per docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md's Artifact Locations section, the comparison ('Compare' stage) artifact lives under `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/`; this protocol names the concrete delta artifact path within that convention as `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta.json`. This feature is protocol-authoring only: no schema (schema/audit_delta.schema.json is listed in NIP-0001's 'Initial schemas' section but product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3 marks audit_delta.schema.json as deferrable/optional within the minimal-viable release -- no feature in the 031-045 execution order is scheduled to author it, so this feature neither creates it nor is blocked by its absence), no tooling, no capability routing or module-index prose beyond the single Module Navigation table-row flip this feature's own deliverable requires (ecosystem/README.md's '07' row, from feature 032's index).
design_notes.planned_document_structure: ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md uses the same house pattern as the shipped ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md and ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (numbered '## N. Title' sections, mirroring methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md): '## 1. Overview', '## 2. Inputs' (states a comparison requires exactly two approved baselines or audits, realizing the 'two approved baselines/audits' input requirement), '## 3. Finding-State Transitions' (names all four transition classes -- new, resolved, reopened, stale -- as the exhaustive classification every tracked finding receives, realizing 'Comparison distinguishes new/resolved/reopened/stale findings'; each of the four state names appears in this section as an inline CODE SPAN -- `new`/`resolved`/`reopened`/`stale`, backtick-delimited, lowercase -- mirroring contract 033's own precedent for the preflight verdict tokens (`PASS`/`PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS`/`FAIL` in ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md Sec 3), NOT bold markdown emphasis -- REVISED per .agent/qa/036-contract-review.json round-0 rejection (HIGH, entries 5 and 10): a bare-word or bold-emphasis convention leaves the per-token substring check exploitable by ordinary English collisions ('renewed' satisfies a bare 'new' check; 'stalemate' satisfies a bare 'stale' check), whereas the backtick-delimited code-span form cannot be satisfied by any English word that merely contains the token's letters, since the required match includes the literal backtick delimiters themselves), '## 4. Closure-Only-With-Evidence Rule' (states the literal phrase 'closure evidence' and a 'MUST NOT' constraint against closing on absence alone), '## 5. Stale-Evidence Non-Reuse Rule' (states the literal phrase 'MUST NOT be silently reused' plus a lowercase, non-sentence-initial, backtick-delimited code-span occurrence of `stale` -- REVISED per the same round-0 finding: a bare lowercase 'stale' token is satisfied by the unrelated word 'stalemate', so the check is anchored to the backtick-delimited form instead), '## 6. Score-Movement Traceability' (states 'traceable' and 'evidence' literally, realizing 'Score movement is traceable to evidence'), '## 7. Comparison Artifact' (states the literal path convention `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta.json`, and a forward prose reference to a not-yet-authored schema/audit_delta.schema.json as optional/deferred per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3 -- distinct from 01/02's forward-references to schemas that WERE committed mandatory-first-release scope), '## 8. References' (points back to the NIP, forward to ecosystem/README.md, and to 02_evidence_baseline.md and 03_engineering_audit.md as the preceding lifecycle steps a comparison measures between). Frontmatter: id 'nizam-ecosystem-progress-comparison', status 'active', version '0.1.0' (first cut, matching 01/02's own 0.1.0), authoritative_source 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' (self-referential house pattern). This structure is committed to here because the anchored, section-scoped verification entries below reference these exact heading strings verbatim; the generator's implementation step MUST use them verbatim or the section-anchored verification commands will legitimately fail (a heading-text mismatch is a defect to fix, per Circuit Breaker attempt 1, not a scope violation). A companion one-line edit to ecosystem/README.md's Module Navigation table flips only the '| 07 | ... | Planned |' row to '| 07 | ... | Shipped |' -- the table itself and its Shipped/Planned convention were authored by feature 032, so this feature edits exactly that one pre-existing cell and nothing else in the file.
design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof: REVISION 1 (this document), rewritten truthfully after .agent/qa/036-contract-review.json's round-0 rejection (HIGH, entries 5 and 10; full text preserved in approvals.round0_rejection below). Round-0 dry-run summary (superseded content, kept for lineage): the section-scoped primitive (tools/verify_lib.sh vlib_section_grep, the same anti-vacuity-approved primitive contracts 031/033/034 used) was dry-run against a GOOD fixture matching the round-0 planned structure (bare/bold word tokens, not code-spans) and a BAD fixture that gutted every section to generic prose; all content-anchored checks appeared to discriminate GOOD-vs-BAD. ROUND-0 DEFECT (caught by the evaluator, not by this dry-run -- the gap this revision closes): the round-0 per-token checks for entries 5 ('new'/'stale' among the four Sec 3 tokens) and 10 (Sec 5's 'stale') used bare, non-word-boundary substring matches. The evaluator built three realistic 3-of-4-correct fixtures -- each fully and correctly naming three of the four required concepts, with the FOURTH never named as a formal class at all, its bare letters merely echoed by an unrelated, plausible English word placed naturally in the same section ('...evidence is renewed' for a missing `new` class; '...must not stalemate the review'/'...must not stalemate the comparison process' for a missing `stale` class, tested independently in both Sec 3 and Sec 5) -- and proved entries 5 and 10 PASS on all three when they should FAIL. The round-0 dry-run never tested this scenario (only a blunt, whole-section-gutted BAD fixture), so its claim that these two checks were 'proven discriminating ... not merely satisfied by any plausible-looking document' was unsupported, exactly as the evaluator found. FIX (per required_fix in both round-0 issues, and per the evaluator's own recommended house pattern: mirror contract 033's backtick-wrapped verdict tokens): the planned document structure (design_notes.planned_document_structure) now commits Sec 3's four transition-class names, and Sec 5's `stale` occurrence, to backtick-delimited inline code spans -- `new`, `resolved`, `reopened`, `stale` -- instead of bare or bold-emphasis words; verification entries 5 and 10 (see their live commands) now search for the backtick-delimited literal forms ('`new`', '`resolved`', '`reopened`', '`stale`') rather than the bare tokens. RE-PROOF (this revision, run fresh against a corrected GOOD fixture and the evaluator's own exact kill-shot scenarios, rebuilt independently in the scratchpad, never committed -- confirmed via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` unchanged before/after this whole re-proof session): (1) GOOD (all four Sec 3 classes and Sec 5's `stale` correctly backtick-wrapped) -- entry 5's live command PASSes (all four backtick tokens found); entry 10's live command PASSes. (2) VACUITY_new (Sec 3 formally names only `resolved`/`reopened`/`stale`; 'new' appears only inside the unrelated bare word 'renewed', never backtick-wrapped) -- entry 5's live command now correctly FAILs ('missing class token: `new`'), closing the exact gap the evaluator demonstrated. (3) VACUITY_stale (Sec 3 formally names only `new`/`resolved`/`reopened`; 'stale' appears only inside the unrelated bare word 'stalemate', never backtick-wrapped) -- entry 5's live command now correctly FAILs ('missing class token: `stale`'). (4) VACUITY_sec5_stale (Sec 5 keeps the 'MUST NOT be silently reused' phrase intact but never backtick-wraps `stale`, only the unrelated bare word 'stalemate' appears) -- entry 10's live command now correctly FAILs. (5) The original round-0 whole-section-gutted BAD fixture was re-run against the corrected entries 5 and 10: still correctly FAILs on every one of the four backtick tokens and on the 'MUST NOT be silently reused' phrase -- the fix did not loosen the check against the original defect class. (6) The original case-sensitivity kill-shot (BAD10_caseflip: Sec 5's only 'stale' occurrence rewritten as capitalized, sentence-initial, non-backtick-wrapped 'Stale evidence...') was re-run against the corrected entry 10 and still correctly FAILs, confirming the case-sensitivity fix from the original dry-run remains intact and is now additionally covered by the backtick-delimiter fix (a capitalized, non-backtick-wrapped occurrence fails on both grounds). All six re-proofs used the exact, unmodified command strings as they now appear in this contract's own verification array (extracted, not retyped), per the same standard the evaluator itself applied. The README row-flip check was dry-run per the round-0 lesson (034.json's) explicitly: a scratch 'before' copy (identical to the real, current ecosystem/README.md, row 07 still 'Planned') and a scratch 'after' copy produced by a single sed substitution changing ONLY the 07 row's last cell (confirmed via `diff -u` showing exactly one changed line, all other Module Navigation rows -- including row 03, currently still 'Planned' in the real tree pending feature 035 -- byte-identical between before/after) were compared: `grep -Fq '| 07 | \`07_progress_comparison.md\` | Compare | Shipped |'` (plain backticks inside single quotes, no stray backslash-escaping, per the exact 034.json round-0 lesson) FAILs against the unmodified real README.md (exit 1, as expected pre-implementation) and PASSes against the flipped scratch copy (exit 0); `git diff --no-index --numstat` between the two scratch copies reports exactly '1\t1' (one line changed), calibrating the numstat one-line-edit guard. IMPORTANT ROW-03 INDEPENDENCE NOTE (per explicit task instruction): this dry-run was run against TODAY's HEAD, where row 03 is still 'Planned' (feature 035 has only contract-drafted, not implemented, as of this proposal). By this feature's own implementation time, the orchestrator serializes README-flipping work so feature 035 will already have implemented AND COMMITTED its own row-03 flip to Shipped -- i.e. HEAD itself will already contain that change before 036's generator starts. Neither the row-07 flip check (a fixed-string match on row 07's own cell only) nor the numstat guard (a diff of the WORKING TREE against WHATEVER HEAD is current at execution time, always exactly 1 insertion + 1 deletion for a single-cell edit regardless of the other seven rows' content) depends on row 03's specific state; both checks are re-runnable byte-for-byte after 035 lands with no revision needed, and the numstat proof above (which used a synthetic before/after pair, not a byte-for-byte snapshot of today's real HEAD) is unaffected by which other Planned/Shipped rows get flipped first. All other (non-content) checks -- scope guard, .agent immutability guard, schema/tools/docs non-goal exclusions -- were additionally dry-run directly against the real, current working tree (see the Generator dispatch's dry-run table), where feature 038 is concurrently IMPLEMENTING (NIZAM.json and schema/README.md modified in the working tree; schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and six tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_*.json fixtures newly untracked; .agent/contracts/038.json, .agent/qa/038-contract-review*.json, and 26 .agent/evidence/038-verify-*.txt files newly untracked) and feature 035 is concurrently CONTRACT-DRAFTING (no .agent/contracts/035.json exists yet on disk at dry-run time); the scope guard and the .agent/qa+.agent/contracts immutability guard, run with this contract's exact allow/exclude lists, both PASS against this live concurrent state right now, proving the calibration is correct against genuine concurrent activity rather than an idealized clean tree.
design_notes.concurrency_fence: Feature 038 (Preflight Verdict Schema + Fixtures) is IMPLEMENTING concurrently in this same working tree. At dry-run time its live, uncommitted footprint is: NIZAM.json (modified, tracked), schema/README.md (modified, tracked), schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json (new, untracked), six tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_*.json fixtures (new, untracked), .agent/contracts/038.json (new, untracked), .agent/qa/038-contract-review*.json (new, untracked), and 26 .agent/evidence/038-verify-*.txt files (new, untracked; evidence files are outside the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard's scope so no exclusion is needed for them). This feature's scope guard (verification entry, vlib_scope_guard) allow-lists 'NIZAM.json', 'schema/README.md', 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json', and the 'tools/fixtures/' prefix SOLELY to tolerate 038's concurrent legitimate edits without a false-positive scope-guard failure -- exactly as contract 034 allow-listed feature 038's earlier-stage footprint, and contract 033 allow-listed feature 032's concurrent edits. Feature 035 (Engineering Audit Protocol) is CONTRACT-DRAFTING concurrently: no .agent/contracts/035.json exists yet at this proposal's dry-run time, but the orchestrator's stated serialization means feature 035 WILL implement and commit its own deliverable (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, and its own ecosystem/README.md '03' row flip) before this feature (036)'s own implementation step begins -- i.e. by the time 036 is implemented, 03_engineering_audit.md already exists in HEAD as a committed, unmodified-by-036 file, and 035's own contract/qa artifacts are also already committed (not live, uncommitted diffs this feature's scope guard would ever see). The .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard (verification entry) nonetheless excludes 035.json/035*.json defensively (in addition to 036's own 036.json/036*.json and 038's 038.json/038*.json) in case any residual 035 contract-negotiation artifact is still uncommitted at the moment this feature's checks run, mirroring the same defensive-exclusion precedent contract 034 applied to feature 038. Because 035's deliverable (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md) will already be a committed, historical file relative to 036's own HEAD by execution time, this feature's non-goal enforcement over 'the other ecosystem/*.md protocol documents' is deliberately split into two disjoint checks rather than one blind existence loop (see non_goals and the two corresponding verification entries): a per-file EXISTENCE guard (must-not-exist) for the docs no feature schedules before or during 036 (00, 04, 05, 06, 08), and a per-file BYTE-IDENTITY-vs-HEAD guard for the docs that already exist or will already exist as prior committed work by 036's execution time (01, 02, 03) -- an existence-must-not-exist check on 03_engineering_audit.md would be a guaranteed false failure once 035 lands first, exactly the failure mode this design avoids per the task's explicit warning.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edits, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happen under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally.

files_create.purpose: The reusable progress-comparison protocol: how two approved baselines or audits are compared, distinguishing new / resolved / reopened / stale findings, the closure-only-with-evidence rule for resolved findings, the stale-evidence non-reuse rule, score-movement traceability to evidence, and the machine-readable delta artifact convention `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta.json`. UDS frontmatter per the house pattern (see design_notes.planned_document_structure).

files_modify.purpose: One-line Module Navigation table update: flip the pre-existing '07' row (`07_progress_comparison.md` / Compare) from 'Planned' to 'Shipped' now that this feature lands the file. No other edit to this file (table structure, other rows, and all other prose are feature 032's authored content and are left byte-for-byte intact except this one cell).

non_goals: No creation or edit of schema/audit_delta.schema.json (deferred/optional per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3; no feature in the 031-045 execution order is scheduled to author it) or any other schema/*.json file except the tolerated concurrent feature 038 surfaces (schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md)

No creation or edit of any file under tools/ except tolerating feature 038's concurrent tools/fixtures/* additions -- deterministic tooling is features 041/042's scope; this feature authors protocol prose only

No edit to tools/skill.json or tools/SKILL.md -- capability routing is feature 040's scope. NIZAM.json is excluded from this non-goal (tolerated feature 038 concurrent surface) rather than asserted byte-identical, since 038 legitimately indexes its own schema there during this feature's window

No creation or edit of any other ecosystem/*.md protocol document that is not yet scheduled to exist (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, 04_dependency_reconciliation.md, 05_release_train_coordination.md, 06_simplification_review.md, 08_ga_gate.md) -- those are successor-phase-deferred protocols per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3

No edit to ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (feature 033's shipped deliverable), ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md (feature 034's shipped deliverable), or ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md (feature 035's deliverable, expected already committed to HEAD by this feature's own execution time per the concurrency_fence design note) -- each remains byte-identical to HEAD, checked per-file, not as a directory-wide assertion

No edit to ecosystem/README.md beyond the single '07' row Shipped/Planned cell -- the table structure, the Canonical Lifecycle prose, the Capability Routing section, and the Consumer Convention section are feature 032's authored content and stay byte-for-byte intact
No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- no planning-artifact or debt changes are authorized by this feature

No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 036.json (tolerating concurrently drafted 035.json and concurrently implementing 038.json), any .agent/qa/*.json (same tolerances, per-file glob-excluded, never a directory-wide assertion), or any feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator
No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false

verification.acceptance_test: F-006/AT: the protocol doc file exists.
verification.command: test -f ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity master proof, mirroring the 031/033/034 fixture-probe precedent): ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md does not exist at HEAD, so every content-anchored check below is proven non-vacuous by construction -- nothing at HEAD can satisfy them because the target artifact itself does not yet exist there.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md already exists at HEAD -- content checks below would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-006/AT: frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json.
verification.command: python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter invalid"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (house-pattern frontmatter fields): id is 'nizam-ecosystem-progress-comparison', status is 'active', and authoritative_source is self-referential.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=open('ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); ok=(fm.get('id')=='nizam-ecosystem-progress-comparison' and fm.get('status')=='active' and fm.get('authoritative_source')=='ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md'); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter house-pattern fields incorrect"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-006/AT 'Comparison distinguishes new/resolved/reopened/stale findings' (STRENGTHENED from feature_list_005.json's coarse whole-file check per the anti-vacuity mandate -- section-anchored, not vacuous; REVISED per .agent/qa/036-contract-review.json round-0 rejection, HIGH: the four class names are anchored to their backtick-delimited inline-code-span form -- `new`/`resolved`/`reopened`/`stale` -- rather than bare words, so an unrelated English word merely containing the same letters, e.g. 'renewed' or 'stalemate', cannot satisfy the check): the '## 3. Finding-State Transitions' section names all four transition classes as backtick-delimited code spans: `new`, `resolved`, `reopened`, and `stale`.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; for tok in '`new`' '`resolved`' '`reopened`' '`stale`'; do vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 3\. Finding-State Transitions$' "$tok" || { echo "Finding-State Transitions section missing class token: $tok"; exit 1; }; done; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-006/AT 'Score movement is traceable to evidence' (STRENGTHENED, section-anchored): the '## 6. Score-Movement Traceability' section states 'traceable' and 'evidence' literally.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 6\. Score-Movement Traceability$' 'traceable' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 6\. Score-Movement Traceability$' 'evidence' && echo PASS || { echo "Score-Movement Traceability section missing 'traceable' or 'evidence'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-006/AT (feature_list_005.json entry 3, verbatim whole-file check): the protocol doc names the four finding-transition classes anywhere in the document, case-insensitively.
verification.command: python3 -c "t=open('ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md',encoding='utf-8').read().lower(); import sys; sys.exit(0 if all(k in t for k in ['new','resolved','reopened','stale']) else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "protocol doc missing one of the four finding-transition classes"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (inputs, per NIP-0001's Compare stage definition -- 'a Compare with no baseline on one or both sides is not a valid comparison'): the '## 2. Inputs' section states a comparison requires two approved baselines or audits.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 2\. Inputs$' 'two approved' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 2\. Inputs$' 'baseline' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 2\. Inputs$' 'audit' && echo PASS || { echo "Inputs section missing 'two approved' / 'baseline' / 'audit'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (closure-only-with-evidence rule, per the task's explicit acceptance core): the '## 4. Closure-Only-With-Evidence Rule' section states the literal phrase 'closure evidence' and a 'MUST NOT' constraint.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 4\. Closure-Only-With-Evidence Rule$' 'closure evidence' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 4\. Closure-Only-With-Evidence Rule$' 'MUST NOT' && echo PASS || { echo "Closure-Only-With-Evidence Rule section missing 'closure evidence' or 'MUST NOT'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (stale-evidence non-reuse rule, per the task's explicit acceptance core; REVISED per .agent/qa/036-contract-review.json round-0 rejection, HIGH: 'stale' is anchored to its backtick-delimited inline-code-span form -- `stale` -- rather than the bare word, so an unrelated English word merely containing the same letters, e.g. 'stalemate', cannot satisfy the check): the '## 5. Stale-Evidence Non-Reuse Rule' section states the literal phrase 'MUST NOT be silently reused' and a backtick-delimited `stale` code span (lowercase, non-sentence-initial occurrence required per this contract's own dry-run case-sensitivity fix -- see design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof).
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 5\. Stale-Evidence Non-Reuse Rule$' 'MUST NOT be silently reused' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 5\. Stale-Evidence Non-Reuse Rule$' '`stale`' && echo PASS || { echo "Stale-Evidence Non-Reuse Rule section missing 'MUST NOT be silently reused' or backtick-delimited \`stale\`"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (machine-readable delta artifact convention, per the NIP's 'Artifact Locations' section and the task's explicit .agent/audits/<audit-id>/ requirement): the '## 7. Comparison Artifact' section states the literal path convention `.agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta.json`.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md '^## 7\. Comparison Artifact$' '\.agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta\.json' && echo PASS || { echo "Comparison Artifact section missing the .agent/audits/<audit-id>/delta.json convention"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (README one-line flip, scoped to exactly the '07' row): ecosystem/README.md's Module Navigation table now marks the 07 row 'Shipped'.
verification.command: grep -Fq '| 07 | `07_progress_comparison.md` | Compare | Shipped |' ecosystem/README.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md 07 row not flipped to Shipped"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (README one-line-only edit guard, dry-run proven against before/after scratch copies, robust to whichever prior rows -- e.g. feature 035's row 03 -- have already flipped by execution time): ecosystem/README.md's diff against HEAD is exactly one inserted and one deleted line.
verification.command: out=$(git diff --numstat HEAD -- ecosystem/README.md); [ "$out" = "$(printf '1\t1\tecosystem/README.md')" ] && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md diff is not exactly 1 insertion + 1 deletion: [$out]"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-006/AT: compliance validator stays green.
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (scope guard, untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant per design_notes.concurrency_fence -- NIZAM.json, schema/README.md, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, and tools/fixtures/ are feature 038's concurrently in-flight scope, and v0.6.0-release-notes.md is a pre-existing untracked artifact this feature does not touch): only this feature's own new/modified files, plus the tolerated concurrent/pre-existing paths, changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' 'ecosystem/README.md' 'NIZAM.json' 'schema/README.md' 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' 'tools/fixtures/' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (.agent/ immutability guard, mirroring the precedent in contracts 025/028/031/033/034, extended per design_notes.concurrency_fence to tolerate feature 035's concurrently drafted contract and feature 038's concurrently implemented contract/qa artifacts, and per amendments[0] to additionally tolerate feature 037's concurrently drafted contract/qa artifacts discovered live at implementation time): no historical (pre-existing, already-approved) .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact was edited -- excludes only this feature's own new 036.json contract and 036*.json qa/review artifacts, feature 035's 035.json contract and 035*.json qa/review artifacts, feature 037's 037.json contract and 037*.json qa/review artifacts, and feature 038's 038.json contract and 038*.json qa/review artifacts.
verification.command: changed=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts ':(exclude).agent/contracts/036.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/036*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/035.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/035*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/037.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/037*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/038.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/038*.json'); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo 'immutable audit record(s) touched:'; echo "$changed"; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, deferred-schema guard): schema/audit_delta.schema.json (deferred/optional per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3; no scheduled feature authors it) does not exist -- this feature performs zero schema work of its own.
verification.command: test -f schema/audit_delta.schema.json && { echo "schema/audit_delta.schema.json already exists -- out of this feature's scope"; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per-file existence loop, scoped only to the docs NOT scheduled to exist by this feature's execution time -- 00/04/05/06/08; deliberately excludes 03_engineering_audit.md, which feature 035 will have already committed to HEAD by this feature's execution time per design_notes.concurrency_fence, and for which a must-not-exist check would be a guaranteed false failure): no successor-phase-deferred ecosystem/*.md protocol document was created by this feature.
verification.command: bad=0; for f in ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md; do if [ -e "$f" ]; then echo "out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per-file byte-identity loop per the task's explicit 'scope byte-identity non-goals per-file, not whole-directory' instruction -- covers the docs already shipped (01, 02) and the doc expected already-shipped-by-execution-time (03, per feature 035's serialized commit), plus CHANGELOG.md): each of these files, checked individually rather than as a directory-wide assertion, remains byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: bad=0; for f in ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md CHANGELOG.md; do if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- "$f"; then echo "out-of-scope file modified: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per-file): tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, and docs/planning/DEBT.md, checked individually, remain byte-identical to HEAD. NIZAM.json is deliberately excluded from this list (see non_goals) since it is feature 038's tolerated concurrent surface.
verification.command: bad=0; for f in tools/skill.json tools/SKILL.md docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md docs/planning/DEBT.md; do if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- "$f"; then echo "out-of-scope file modified: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/036-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/036-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/036-verify-20.txt for the twentieth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 20 entries). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

amendments.change: Added ':(exclude).agent/contracts/037.json' and ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/037*.json' to the guard's git status pathspec excludes, alongside the already-approved 035/036/038 excludes.
amendments.rationale: At Loop-2 implementation time (post feature-035 commit at HEAD 4b01290, per the orchestrator's dispatch), feature 037 (Baseline Schema + Fixtures) is concurrently drafting its own contract (.agent/contracts/037.json, untracked) -- a sibling still in Mode A, not touched by this feature. Round-0 and rev1 contract negotiation for 036 only anticipated concurrent siblings 035 and 038 (the features live at those review times); 037's concurrent drafting was not yet observable and so was not in the original exclude list. Running the guard's live, unmodified command against the real tree at implementation time correctly and legitimately flagged .agent/contracts/037.json as 'touched' (it is genuinely new since HEAD), which is the guard behaving exactly as designed against an input its exclude list did not yet anticipate -- not a logic defect in the guard's git-status/exclude mechanism itself, but a scope gap in which siblings were pre-declared tolerated. This mirrors the exact precedent contract 019's own amendments[0] and contract 034's original design already established for other concurrent siblings (035/038): extend the exclude list to the additional legitimate, independently-scoped, currently-in-flight sibling, per AH-2 (the underlying problem -- a real, uncommitted 037.json this feature does not own or touch -- still exists and is not a false alarm to be silently ignored, but a scope-tolerance gap to be explicitly declared).
amendments.dry_run_proof: Before this amendment: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts` (excluding only 035/036/038 patterns) returned exactly one line, '?? .agent/contracts/037.json', causing exit 1. After this amendment (037.json/037*.json added to the exclude pathspec): the same live command returns empty and exits 0. The exclude patterns for 037 are byte-identical in form to the already-approved 035 and 038 patterns (':(exclude).agent/contracts/037.json' and ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/037*.json'), so the guard's discriminating power against a GENUINE immutable-artifact edit is unchanged: any .agent/qa or .agent/contracts path other than 035/036/037/038's own would still be caught, exactly as before this amendment.
amendments.ratification_note: Ratified by validator Mode B 2026-07-17 (verdict field amendment_ratified: true; diff-vs-rev1 confirmed the amendment + approvals flip only; intent judged preserved -- excludes match the approved 035/038 pattern forms). Field reconciled by orchestrator after evaluator qa/036.json flagged the stale false value as an unsupported claim.

findings: HIGH (verification[4]/entry 5): the Sec 3 finding-state-transition per-token loop used bare, non-word-boundary-anchored substring tokens ('new', 'resolved', 'reopened', 'stale'). The evaluator built realistic 3-of-4-correct fixtures where the omitted class's letters were echoed only by an unrelated word in ordinary prose ('renewed' for 'new'; 'stalemate' for 'stale') and proved the check PASSes when it should FAIL.

HIGH (verification[9]/entry 10): the Sec 5 'stale' check had the same bare-substring defect, independently reproduced with a 'stalemate' decoy that leaves the section's substantive 'a finding is flagged as stale' claim entirely unproven while the check still PASSes.

Consequently: missing_acceptance_coverage (F-006/AT 'new'/'stale' classes unproven) and an unsupported_claims finding against design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof's original (round-0) discrimination claim, which only tested a blunt whole-section-gutted BAD fixture, never the realistic 3-of-4 scenario.
round0_rejection.fixed_in: revision 1 (this document): design_notes.planned_document_structure now commits Sec 3's four transition-class names and Sec 5's 'stale' occurrence to backtick-delimited inline code spans (`new`/`resolved`/`reopened`/`stale`), mirroring contract 033's own precedent for the preflight verdict tokens; verification entries 5 and 10 now match the backtick-delimited literal forms, which cannot be satisfied by an unrelated word merely containing the same letters (no English word is delimited by backticks around 'new' or 'stale' by coincidence). Re-proven against the evaluator's own exact kill-shot scenarios (VACUITY_new, VACUITY_stale, VACUITY_sec5_stale) plus the original whole-gutted BAD fixture and the original case-flip fixture: all now correctly FAIL; a corrected GOOD fixture correctly PASSes both entries. design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof restated truthfully from this actual re-run.
